mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary pressure, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 3.0 percent annually, or interaction term IC is negative over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percent in a single month, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening economic activity that directly boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 250.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated central bank tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for nominal yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized labor and capital investment expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when HOUST declines while PAYEMS rises, or the interaction effect yields a negative IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll additions fall below 100,000, weakening labor demand signals an impending economic slowdown that reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 70% of instances where PAYEMS growth drops below 100,000, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 2-year test period.
